input.query: Which note is about porous pots feeding moisture underground to garden plants?
documents: Tomato seedlings thrived after the grower switched to clay ollas that release water slowly near roots. The buried jars kept leaves dry and cut watering trips during the heat wave.

During storms, runoff soaked into planted basins beside the curb after the parking lot retrofit. Inspectors described the planted depressions as shallow rain gardens that slow street water, feed sedges and switchgrass, and keep oil grit from rushing into the drain.
